patch.value:
## Agent need
Agent-driven churn can silently erode code quality; only ratcheted, machine-enforced gates keep the codebase maintainable as many contributors extend it.

## Delivered surface (pm-92if)
Strict ESLint + eslint-suppressions ratchet (168->109); jscpd duplication gate; LOC + cyclomatic-complexity caps; god-function decomposition precedent (runCreate 172->17).

## Verified by
Static gates in CI (CI Gates). In-progress epic; residual decompositions tracked by open children (pm-ueuq, pm-jt3b, pm-f4yn).
As a maintainer extending pm with agents, I want CodeFactor A+ enforced by ratcheted static gates, so that the codebase stays maintainable no matter how many agents contribute

eslint-suppressions.json budget (<=17/file) is the A+ source of truth and only ratchets down; static complexity cap 260 and per-function CC budget hold; LOC cap 3400/file; jscpd duplication gate holds; zero outstanding CodeFactor findings.
In progress under pm-92if: CodeFactor driven A->A+ with suppressions ratcheted 168->109; remaining decompositions tracked by open children.
The codebase stays A+ maintainable under heavy agent contribution.
Static gates + suppression ratchet in place; A+ target with residual findings tracked.
In progress under pm-92if: CodeFactor driven A->A+ with suppressions ratcheted 168->109; remaining decompositions tracked by open children.

author: claude-code-agent

patch.value: Capability story delivered by pm-92if; residual quality work remains tracked by open children.
